text:
{
  "instructions":"Time Allowed: 3 hours 15 minutes (15 minutes reading and 3 hours writing). This examination has two sections; A & B. Section A has one compulsory question to be attempted. Section B has four questions, three questions to be attempted. Marks allocated to each question are shown at the end of the question. Show all your workings. Any assumptions made must be clearly and concisely stated.",
   "answerKey":{

   },
   "allowSelectiveAnswering":true,
   "sectionBRequiredQuestions":3,
   "sectionCRequiredQuestions":1,
   "sections":[
      {
         "name":"A",
         "description":"This section has one compulsory question",
         "questions":[
            {
               "questionNumber":1,
               "text":"Explain the purpose of the following financial statements: i) Statement of financial position (2 marks) ii) Income statement (2 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "subQuestions":[
                  {
                     "label":"a) i)",
                     "text":"Statement of financial position",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "correctAnswer":"The statement of financial position provides a snapshot of a company's financial position at a specific point in time, including its assets, liabilities, and equity.",
                     "points":2
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"a) ii)",
                     "text":"Income statement",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "correctAnswer":"The income statement provides a summary of a company's revenues and expenses over a specific period of time, and is used to calculate the company's net income.",
                     "points":2
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"b) i)",
                     "text":"An expense account",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "correctAnswer":"A debit entry in an expense account represents an increase in expenses.",
                     "points":1
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"b) ii)",
                     "text":"A liability account",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "correctAnswer":"A debit entry in a liability account represents a decrease in liabilities.",
                     "points":1
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"b) iii)",
                     "text":"An asset account",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "correctAnswer":"A debit entry in an asset account represents an increase in assets.",
                     "points":1
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"b) iv)",
                     "text":"An income account",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "correctAnswer":"A debit entry in an income account represents a decrease in income.",
                     "points":1
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"b) v)",
                     "text":"In October 2012 Diana issued a credit note for Frw 95,000 to a customer, but this was omitted from her accounting records.",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "correctAnswer":"The general journal entry to record this transaction would be: Debit Accounts Receivable Frw 95,000, Credit Sales Returns Frw 95,000.",
                     "points":2
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"c) i) Income statement for the year ended 30 September 2012",
                     "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
                     "passage":"The following account balances were extracted from the books of Linett at the end of her financial year 30 September 2012: | Account | Frw | Sales | 5,400,000 | Purchases | 2,826,000 | Shop fittings cost | 2,340,000 | Accumulated depreciation – shop fittings | 240,000 | Capital | 3,060,000 | Opening inventory | 846,000 | Bank as per cashbook | 90,000 | Cash | 18,000 | Shop wages | 792,000 | Accounts receivable | 456,000 | Drawings | 630,000 | Accounts payable | 90,000 | Carriage in | 36,000 | Carriage out | 27,000 | Maintenance and repair | 135,000 | Commission income | 180,000 | Electricity and water | 144,000 | Rent income | 126,000 | Insurance | 810,000 | Allowance for doubtful debt | 54,000 | 9,150,000 | 9,150,000 | Additional information: 1. The closing inventory cost and net realizable amount was Frw 910,000 and Frw 890,000 respectively. 2. Bank interest income accrued Frw 80,000 was only shown in the bank statement.",
                     "points":17,
                     "spreadsheetTemplate":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Income Statement\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Sales\",\"\"],[\"Cost of Sales\",\"\"],[\"Gross Profit\",\"\"],[\"Net Profit\",\"\"]]},{\"title\":\"Balance Sheet\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Total Assets\",\"\"],[\"Total Liabilities and Capital\",\"\"]]}]}",
                     "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Income Statement\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Sales\",\"5,400,000\"],[\"Cost of Sales\",\"2,762,000\"],[\"Gross Profit\",\"2,638,000\"],[\"Net Profit\",\"1,222,000\"]]},{\"title\":\"Balance Sheet\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Total Assets\",\"3,480,000\"],[\"Total Liabilities and Capital\",\"3,480,000\"]]}]}",
                     "correctAnswer":"See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Income Statement and Balance Sheet."
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"c) ii) Balance sheet as at 30 September 2012",
                     "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
                     "passage":"The following account balances were extracted from the books of Linett at the end of her financial year 30 September 2012: | Account | Frw | Sales | 5,400,000 | Purchases | 2,826,000 | Shop fittings cost | 2,340,000 | Accumulated depreciation – shop fittings | 240,000 | Capital | 3,060,000 | Opening inventory | 846,000 | Bank as per cashbook | 90,000 | Cash | 18,000 | Shop wages | 792,000 | Accounts receivable | 456,000 | Drawings | 630,000 | Accounts payable | 90,000 | Carriage in | 36,000 | Carriage out | 27,000 | Maintenance and repair | 135,000 | Commission income | 180,000 | Electricity and water | 144,000 | Rent income | 126,000 | Insurance | 810,000 | Allowance for doubtful debt | 54,000 | 9,150,000 | 9,150,000 | Additional information: 1. The closing inventory cost and net realizable amount was Frw 910,000 and Frw 890,000 respectively. 2. Bank interest income accrued Frw 80,000 was only shown in the bank statement.",
                     "points":13,
                     "spreadsheetTemplate":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Income Statement\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Sales\",\"\"],[\"Cost of Sales\",\"\"],[\"Gross Profit\",\"\"],[\"Net Profit\",\"\"]]},{\"title\":\"Balance Sheet\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Total Assets\",\"\"],[\"Total Liabilities and Capital\",\"\"]]}]}",
                     "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Income Statement\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Sales\",\"5,400,000\"],[\"Cost of Sales\",\"2,762,000\"],[\"Gross Profit\",\"2,638,000\"],[\"Net Profit\",\"1,222,000\"]]},{\"title\":\"Balance Sheet\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Total Assets\",\"3,480,000\"],[\"Total Liabilities and Capital\",\"3,480,000\"]]}]}",
                     "correctAnswer":"See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Income Statement and Balance Sheet."
                  }
               ],
               "points":40
            }
         ]
      },
      {
         "name":"B",
         "description":"Attempt three of the four questions in this section.",
         "questions":[
            {
               "questionNumber":2,
               "text":"State and explain the four causes of difference between the cash book balance and the bank statement balance that need to be dealt with in the bank reconciliation statement.",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "subQuestions":[
                  {
                     "label":"a)",
                     "text":"State and explain the four causes of difference between the cash book balance and the bank statement balance",
                     "type":"open-ended",
                     "correctAnswer":"The four causes of difference between the cash book balance and the bank statement balance are: 1) Cheques issued but not yet presented to the bank, 2) Cheques deposited but not yet cleared, 3) Bank charges and interest, and 4) Errors in the cash book or bank statement.",
                     "points":4
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"b) i) Adjusted cashbook (bank column)",
                     "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
                     "passage":"The cash book of John showed credit balance of Frw 30,850 on 30 September 2012 whereas the bank statement showed debit balance of Frw 30,250 on the same date.",
                     "points":10,
                     "spreadsheetTemplate":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Adjusted Cashbook\",\"headers\":[\"Date\",\"Particulars\",\"Debit\",\"Credit\"],\"data\":[[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"]]}]}",
                     "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Adjusted Cashbook\",\"headers\":[\"Date\",\"Particulars\",\"Debit\",\"Credit\"],\"data\":[[\"\",\"\",\"30,250\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"]]}]}",
                     "correctAnswer":"See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Adjusted Cashbook."
                  },
                  {
                     "label":"b) ii) Bank reconciliation statement",
                     "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
                     "passage":"The cash book of John showed credit balance of Frw 30,850 on 30 September 2012 whereas the bank statement showed debit balance of Frw 30,250 on the same date.",
                     "points":6,
                     "spreadsheetTemplate":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Bank Reconciliation Statement\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Debit\",\"Credit\"],\"data\":[[\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\"]]}]}",
                     "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Bank Reconciliation Statement\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Debit\",\"Credit\"],\"data\":[[\"\",\"30,250\"],[\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\"]]}]}",
                     "correctAnswer":"See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Bank Reconciliation Statement."
                  }
               ],
               "points":20
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":3,
               "text":"Prepare an Income Statement for the year ended 30 September 2012 and a Statement of Financial position as at 30 September 2012.",
               "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
               "passage":"The following list of balances as at 30 September 2012 has been extracted from the books of Ocampo and Hague, trading partnership, sharing the balance of profits and losses in the proportions 3:2 respectively.",
               "points":20,
               "spreadsheetTemplate":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Income Statement\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Sales\",\"\"],[\"Cost of Sales\",\"\"],[\"Gross Profit\",\"\"],[\"Net Profit\",\"\"]]},{\"title\":\"Balance Sheet\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Total Assets\",\"\"],[\"Total Liabilities and Capital\",\"\"]]}]}",
               "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Income Statement\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Sales\",\"322,100\"],[\"Cost of Sales\",\"208,200\"],[\"Gross Profit\",\"113,900\"],[\"Net Profit\",\"56,950\"]]},{\"title\":\"Balance Sheet\",\"headers\":[\"Item\",\"Frw\"],\"data\":[[\"Total Assets\",\"1,234,500\"],[\"Total Liabilities and Capital\",\"1,234,500\"]]}]}",
               "correctAnswer":"See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Income Statement and Balance Sheet."
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":4,
               "text":"Journal entries to correct errors and omissions and a revised balance sheet as at 31 March 2012.",
               "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
               "passage":"The balance sheet of Joab Utaka, a sole trader, as at 31 March 2012 was as follows: | Account | Frw | Capital | 1,890 | Land and buildings | 1,650 | Profit for year 2012 | 450 | Machinery (at cost) | 1,200 | Deduct: depreciation | 750 | 450 | Creditors | 630 | Stock at cost | 570 | Bank overdraft | 270 | Debtors | 420 | 990 | 3,090 | 3,090 |",
               "points":20,
               "spreadsheetTemplate":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Journal Entries\",\"headers\":[\"Date\",\"Particulars\",\"Debit\",\"Credit\"],\"data\":[[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"]]}]}",
               "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Journal Entries\",\"headers\":[\"Date\",\"Particulars\",\"Debit\",\"Credit\"],\"data\":[[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"]]}]}",
               "correctAnswer":"See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Journal Entries."
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":5,
               "text":"Calculate the Capital on 1 January 2011, enter the transactions in the ledger accounts and a three column cash book, and draft a trial balance at 31 January 2011.",
               "type":"financial-spreadsheet",
               "passage":"The following balances were extracted from the books of Ejide Kalisa a business man at Nyabugogo Bus Park, as at 1st January 2011: | Account | Frw | Fixed assets | 1,750,000 | Stock in trade | 294,000 | Trade debtors | 420,000 | Trade creditors | 315,000 | Balances at bank | 252,000 | Cash balance | 84,000 | Bank loan | 840,000 | Accrued sundry expenses | 70,000 |",
               "points":20,
               "spreadsheetTemplate":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Ledger Accounts\",\"headers\":[\"Date\",\"Particulars\",\"Debit\",\"Credit\"],\"data\":[[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"]]}]}",
               "spreadsheetModelAnswer":"{\"tables\":[{\"title\":\"Ledger Accounts\",\"headers\":[\"Date\",\"Particulars\",\"Debit\",\"Credit\"],\"data\":[[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"],[\"\",\"\",\"\"]]}]}",
               "correctAnswer":"See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Ledger Accounts."
            }
         ]
      }
   ]
}

parsedContent.instructions: Time Allowed: 3 hours 15 minutes (15 minutes reading and 3 hours writing). This examination has two sections; A & B. Section A has one compulsory question to be attempted. Section B has four questions, three questions to be attempted. Marks allocated to each question are shown at the end of the question. Show all your workings. Any assumptions made must be clearly and concisely stated.

sections.name: A
sections.description: This section has one compulsory question

questions.text: Explain the purpose of the following financial statements: i) Statement of financial position (2 marks) ii) Income statement (2 marks)

subQuestions.label: a) i)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: The statement of financial position provides a snapshot of a company's financial position at a specific point in time, including its assets, liabilities, and equity.

subQuestions.label: a) ii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: The income statement provides a summary of a company's revenues and expenses over a specific period of time, and is used to calculate the company's net income.

subQuestions.label: b) i)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: A debit entry in an expense account represents an increase in expenses.

subQuestions.label: b) ii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: A debit entry in a liability account represents a decrease in liabilities.

subQuestions.label: b) iii)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: A debit entry in an asset account represents an increase in assets.

subQuestions.label: b) iv)
subQuestions.correctAnswer: A debit entry in an income account represents a decrease in income.

subQuestions.label: b) v)
subQuestions.text: In October 2012 Diana issued a credit note for Frw 95,000 to a customer, but this was omitted from her accounting records.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: The general journal entry to record this transaction would be: Debit Accounts Receivable Frw 95,000, Credit Sales Returns Frw 95,000.

subQuestions.label: c) i) Income statement for the year ended 30 September 2012
subQuestions.passage: The following account balances were extracted from the books of Linett at the end of her financial year 30 September 2012: | Account | Frw | Sales | 5,400,000 | Purchases | 2,826,000 | Shop fittings cost | 2,340,000 | Accumulated depreciation – shop fittings | 240,000 | Capital | 3,060,000 | Opening inventory | 846,000 | Bank as per cashbook | 90,000 | Cash | 18,000 | Shop wages | 792,000 | Accounts receivable | 456,000 | Drawings | 630,000 | Accounts payable | 90,000 | Carriage in | 36,000 | Carriage out | 27,000 | Maintenance and repair | 135,000 | Commission income | 180,000 | Electricity and water | 144,000 | Rent income | 126,000 | Insurance | 810,000 | Allowance for doubtful debt | 54,000 | 9,150,000 | 9,150,000 | Additional information: 1. The closing inventory cost and net realizable amount was Frw 910,000 and Frw 890,000 respectively. 2. Bank interest income accrued Frw 80,000 was only shown in the bank statement.
subQuestions.spreadsheetTemplate: {"tables":[{"title":"Income Statement","headers":["Item","Frw"],"data":[["Sales",""],["Cost of Sales",""],["Gross Profit",""],["Net Profit",""]]},{"title":"Balance Sheet","headers":["Item","Frw"],"data":[["Total Assets",""],["Total Liabilities and Capital",""]]}]}
subQuestions.spreadsheetModelAnswer: {"tables":[{"title":"Income Statement","headers":["Item","Frw"],"data":[["Sales","5,400,000"],["Cost of Sales","2,762,000"],["Gross Profit","2,638,000"],["Net Profit","1,222,000"]]},{"title":"Balance Sheet","headers":["Item","Frw"],"data":[["Total Assets","3,480,000"],["Total Liabilities and Capital","3,480,000"]]}]}
subQuestions.correctAnswer: See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Income Statement and Balance Sheet.

subQuestions.label: c) ii) Balance sheet as at 30 September 2012
subQuestions.passage: The following account balances were extracted from the books of Linett at the end of her financial year 30 September 2012: | Account | Frw | Sales | 5,400,000 | Purchases | 2,826,000 | Shop fittings cost | 2,340,000 | Accumulated depreciation – shop fittings | 240,000 | Capital | 3,060,000 | Opening inventory | 846,000 | Bank as per cashbook | 90,000 | Cash | 18,000 | Shop wages | 792,000 | Accounts receivable | 456,000 | Drawings | 630,000 | Accounts payable | 90,000 | Carriage in | 36,000 | Carriage out | 27,000 | Maintenance and repair | 135,000 | Commission income | 180,000 | Electricity and water | 144,000 | Rent income | 126,000 | Insurance | 810,000 | Allowance for doubtful debt | 54,000 | 9,150,000 | 9,150,000 | Additional information: 1. The closing inventory cost and net realizable amount was Frw 910,000 and Frw 890,000 respectively. 2. Bank interest income accrued Frw 80,000 was only shown in the bank statement.
subQuestions.spreadsheetTemplate: {"tables":[{"title":"Income Statement","headers":["Item","Frw"],"data":[["Sales",""],["Cost of Sales",""],["Gross Profit",""],["Net Profit",""]]},{"title":"Balance Sheet","headers":["Item","Frw"],"data":[["Total Assets",""],["Total Liabilities and Capital",""]]}]}
subQuestions.spreadsheetModelAnswer: {"tables":[{"title":"Income Statement","headers":["Item","Frw"],"data":[["Sales","5,400,000"],["Cost of Sales","2,762,000"],["Gross Profit","2,638,000"],["Net Profit","1,222,000"]]},{"title":"Balance Sheet","headers":["Item","Frw"],"data":[["Total Assets","3,480,000"],["Total Liabilities and Capital","3,480,000"]]}]}
subQuestions.correctAnswer: See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Income Statement and Balance Sheet.

sections.name: B
sections.description: Attempt three of the four questions in this section.

questions.text: State and explain the four causes of difference between the cash book balance and the bank statement balance that need to be dealt with in the bank reconciliation statement.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: State and explain the four causes of difference between the cash book balance and the bank statement balance
subQuestions.correctAnswer: The four causes of difference between the cash book balance and the bank statement balance are: 1) Cheques issued but not yet presented to the bank, 2) Cheques deposited but not yet cleared, 3) Bank charges and interest, and 4) Errors in the cash book or bank statement.

subQuestions.label: b) i) Adjusted cashbook (bank column)
subQuestions.passage: The cash book of John showed credit balance of Frw 30,850 on 30 September 2012 whereas the bank statement showed debit balance of Frw 30,250 on the same date.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Adjusted Cashbook.

subQuestions.label: b) ii) Bank reconciliation statement
subQuestions.passage: The cash book of John showed credit balance of Frw 30,850 on 30 September 2012 whereas the bank statement showed debit balance of Frw 30,250 on the same date.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Bank Reconciliation Statement.

questions.text: Prepare an Income Statement for the year ended 30 September 2012 and a Statement of Financial position as at 30 September 2012.
questions.passage: The following list of balances as at 30 September 2012 has been extracted from the books of Ocampo and Hague, trading partnership, sharing the balance of profits and losses in the proportions 3:2 respectively.

questions.correctAnswer: See spreadsheetModelAnswer for the completed Journal Entries.

questions.text: Calculate the Capital on 1 January 2011, enter the transactions in the ledger accounts and a three column cash book, and draft a trial balance at 31 January 2011.
questions.passage: The following balances were extracted from the books of Ejide Kalisa a business man at Nyabugogo Bus Park, as at 1st January 2011: | Account | Frw | Fixed assets | 1,750,000 | Stock in trade | 294,000 | Trade debtors | 420,000 | Trade creditors | 315,000 | Balances at bank | 252,000 | Cash balance | 84,000 | Bank loan | 840,000 | Accrued sundry expenses | 70,000 |
